description:
**Problem**
beads-rs needs wisps - ephemeral molecules that never sync to JSONL. Wisps are crucial for operational workflows (patrols, releases, health checks) that shouldn't clutter git history.

**What are Wisps?**
Wisps are ephemeral issues with Ephemeral=true flag:
- Created: bd mol wisp <proto> or bd create --ephemeral
- Stored: In SQLite (.beads/beads.db) with Ephemeral=true
- Excluded: From JSONL export (never sync to git)
- Lifecycle: Either squash to digest OR burn without trace
- Use cases: Patrol cycles, release workflows, health checks, diagnostics

**How it Works (Go beads)**
1. Create wisp: Sets Ephemeral=true on all spawned issues
2. Execution: Normal bd operations work on wisp issues
3. Export filtering: autoflush.go skips issues where Ephemeral=true
4. Cleanup: bd mol squash <id> OR bd mol burn <id>
5. GC: bd mol wisp gc removes orphaned old wisps

**Phase Metaphor**
- Solid (proto): Template
- Liquid (mol): Persistent work via bd pour
- Vapor (wisp): Ephemeral work via bd mol wisp

**Design**
The Ephemeral flag is already in Go Issue struct. In Rust:
1. Add ephemeral: bool to Issue (with Lww<bool>)
2. Export filter: Skip issues where ephemeral.value == true
3. Wisp commands: bd mol wisp create <proto>
4. Lifecycle commands: squash, burn, gc

**Files to Study (Go beads)**
- cmd/bd/wisp.go - wisp creation and management
- cmd/bd/autoflush.go - export filtering (line ~200: skip ephemeral)
- cmd/bd/mol_squash.go - compress wisps to digest
- cmd/bd/mol_burn.go - delete without trace
- docs/MOLECULES.md - vapor phase explanation

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Issue.ephemeral field added (Lww<bool>)
- [ ] Export skips ephemeral issues
- [ ] bd mol wisp create command
- [ ] bd mol wisp list command
- [ ] bd mol wisp gc command
- [ ] bd mol squash compresses wisps
- [ ] bd mol burn deletes wisps
- [ ] Tests for ephemeral lifecycle
title: Add wisp system to beads-rs

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical app config schema/load/merge/write live in `beads-bootstrap`
- [ ] Duplicate top-level `Config` / `PathsConfig` definitions are removed from `beads-daemon`
- [ ] `beads-rs` and `beads-daemon` both consume the same canonical config types
- [ ] Telemetry code uses bootstrap-owned logging config types
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-bootstrap -p beads-rs -p beads-daemon` passes
description:
**Problem**
The canonical app config currently lives under `crates/beads-rs/src/config/**`, but `crates/beads-daemon/src/config.rs` still defines its own duplicate top-level config/path schema alongside daemon runtime policy types. Telemetry code in both crates also depends on those config types.

**Where**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/config/mod.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/config/load.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/config/merge.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/config/schema.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/config.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/telemetry.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/telemetry.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs` — current daemon runtime config mapping

**Why It Matters**
This violates the repo's one-source-of-truth rule. A reader cannot tell whether app config truth lives in `beads-rs` or `beads-daemon`, and changes to config semantics can drift across crates.

**Files to Study**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/config/**`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/config.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/telemetry.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/telemetry.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs`
design: Move the canonical app config schema/load/merge/write pipeline into `beads-bootstrap`. Keep daemon runtime-only policy types in `beads-daemon` (`DaemonRuntimeConfig`, `GitSyncPolicy`, `CheckpointPolicy`) and add an explicit conversion boundary from bootstrap config to daemon runtime config. Do not let `beads-bootstrap` become a daemon policy crate.
title: Move canonical config schema/load/merge into beads-bootstrap and delete daemon config duplication
